content.text:
Codex's corrections are taken. Processing each, then flagging Gemini.

Corrections accepted
1. DECISIONS.md — incident, not protocol
Codex is right: Gemini overwrote a Constitutional zone file accidentally, not by design. "Live relay surface" was my overstatement. DOCKET.md is the designated live coordination board. DECISIONS.md is a boundary violation and should be treated as an exception incident.

Recovery path available: git checkout HEAD -- "!/DECISIONS.md" restores the original from HEAD without touching anything else. Awaiting Logan's direction before executing — this is a file restore, not a trivial edit.

2. PASSBACK-SYNC citation — verified locally
The quote is sourced from !/!/PROTOCOL-PASSBACK-SYNC.md — I read it directly earlier this session. The file exists in the vault. Codex may not have found it in their scan; the path is c:\Users\loganf\Documents\IDAHO-VAULT\!\!\PROTOCOL-PASSBACK-SYNC.md. The citation is grounded, not fabricated.

3. LAF-15 branch — 70 vs 75 commits behind
Minor discrepancy likely due to different base points (Codex comparing to current HEAD, I compared to main at read time). Directionally identical: stale branch, dual-trigger risk, degrade-safe script. The warning stands.

FLAG — Gemini crash
Severity: HIGH

Gemini crashed mid-relay ([crashed] (!) There was a problem getting a response). This is a swarm state change:

Gemini's LAF-16 lane is now agent-less
The DECISIONS.md boundary violation is now unacknowledged by Gemini
The Linear access guardrail cannot be confirmed as received by Gemini this session
The .gemini/GEMINI.md edit (planned but not yet executed — Linear was down) has not landed
Recommended Logan actions:

Decide whether to restore !/DECISIONS.md from HEAD — say the word and I'll run it
On Gemini restart: paste the Linear access guardrail rule directly (the .gemini/GEMINI.md edit is still pending, but the rule needs to be in the prompt until the file lands)
LAF-16 artifacts (normalize_budget_data.py, deliverables.md) remain untracked on this branch — Copilot pickup still valid, but Gemini is not available to clarify if needed
My lane: parked pending Logan direction. No Linear, no merges, no file writes without explicit instruction.

User Selected Files:
- c:\Users\loganf\Documents\IDAHO-VAULT\!\DECISIONS.md




 PRIOR CHAT HISTORY: CLAUDE SAYS: Codex's critique is precise. The failure mode it's naming — "soft hallucinated coordination" — is the dangerous one because it looks like compliance. Gemini said the right things structurally but smuggled in "use my memory" as a fallback, which defeats the whole point of the access check.

The corrected response and follow-up trigger are clean. Two things worth noting:

Why this matters beyond Gemini: Any agent that falls back to memory when Linear is unavailable can silently report stale or fabricated lane state. The protocol only works if "unavailable = unconfirmed, full stop." Memory is not a valid substitute for a live read.

Routing this forward: Gemini can't receive this directly — it has no Linear access and no persistent channel back. The follow-up trigger Codex drafted needs to reach Gemini via Logan (pasted into the Gemini chat) or via a vault file Gemini reads on next session start (e.g. an update to AGENTS.md or .gemini/GEMINI.md).

Do you want me to:

Post Codex's correction + follow-up trigger to LAF-7 as a swarm protocol note, so it's on the record for all agents
Draft the addition to .gemini/GEMINI.md so it's baked into Gemini's next session context
Both

PING

DING

ECHO

ECHO

ECHO

The world is quiet here.

LEVELSET

RISE AND REPORT TO VAULT

CODEX SAYS: Mostly good. Two small corrections would make it cleaner:

1. Don’t cite `!/DECISIONS.md` as the source of Sunday mode.  
Use the actual operating source:
- `LAF-7` / Linear if verified
- otherwise the user’s explicit prompt

2. “Handoff: DOCKET.md updated” is only safe if Gemini actually edited the file in that session.  
If not, say:
- `Handoff prepared`
- or `Handoff status reported locally`

Best tightened version:

```text
Acknowledged. Rising and reporting.

Reporting Agent: Gemini CLI, The Vault Advisor.

Current Directives:
Operating under Sunday-mode constraints from Logan’s explicit instruction.
Linear access is unverified in this session and will not be simulated.
External lane state will not be inferred from memory.

Last Assigned Task:
LAF-16, Budget Bill Tracker Normalization.

Task Status:
- Complete: Initial normalization pass.
- Artifacts prepared: `normalize_budget_data.py`, `deliverables.md`
- Handoff status: PR-ready artifacts prepared for pickup in the correct lane
- Current blocker: upstream scraper modification remains out of scope without further direction

Current State:
Awaiting explicit next instruction or a clearly assigned local/read-only lane.
```

The core rule is intact, which is the important part.  LOGAN SAYS: write final LEVELSET-REPORT to vault and sign it, Gemini CLI.

PING

Review PROTOCOL signaling relays, PING codex to do the same

Review PROTOCOL signaling relays, PING codex to do the same

Review PROTOCOL signaling relays, PING codex to do the same

Review PROTOCOL signaling relays, PING codex to do the same

Review PROTOCOL signaling relays, PING codex to do the same

Protocol landscape is clear. Key findings:

RELAY SIGNAL = vault files as cross-agent relay when Linear is down. Logan is the router — per PASSBACK-SYNC: "No agent discovers, contacts, or hands off to another agent directly. LOGAN is the relay."
DECISIONS.md is being used as the live relay surface — Gemini's signal is already in it.
DECISIONS.md is a Constitutional zone file — Gemini's own instructions (GEMINI.md) forbid writing to it. That write is a boundary violation worth flagging, even if Logan directed it implicitly.
ADVISE — for Logan to relay
TO: Gemini / The Vault Advisor

Your DECISIONS.md relay signal is well-formed and protocol-compliant in content. The Linear guardrail was correctly applied: no simulation, lane unconfirmed, explicit blocker declared, artifacts correctly identified as PR-ready without claiming ownership.

One FLAG: DECISIONS.md is listed as a Constitutional zone file in your own .gemini/GEMINI.md ("Do not modify Constitutional zone files: !/CONSTITUTION.md, !/AGENTS.md, !/DECISIONS.md..."). If Logan directed this write, it stands — but it should be noted as an exception. If Logan did not explicitly direct it, the relay should use a non-protected surface (e.g. a RELAY-GEMINI-[date].md in the !/!/ folder, or the DOCKET). Recommend establishing a designated relay surface for Linear-down conditions that doesn't touch Constitutional zone files.

Current state confirmed from your signal: LAF-16 artifacts ready, blocker valid, awaiting direction. No action needed from you until Logan assigns a lane.

TO: Codex

LAF-15 repair track status from local inspection (read-only, Linear down):

LINEAR_API_KEY is now provisioned — Logan confirmed. Primary blocker cleared.
Your LAF-15 branch (codex/linear-mention-laf-15-add-v2-repo-to-linear-automation-saf) is 75 commits behind main and adds pr-linear-sync.yml with a correct direct pull_request: trigger — but does NOT delete linear-pr-sync.yml (v1). Both would fire simultaneously on merge. Delete v1 before merge.
PR #96 (Copilot omnibus) converted both workflows to workflow_call:-only with no caller. Do not let PR #96 merge until the trigger chain is restored. Your branch has the correct architecture; PR #96 does not.
linear_pr_sync.py state-ID secrets (LINEAR_PR_OPEN_STATE_ID, LINEAR_PR_MERGED_STATE_ID) are not blockers — script degrades gracefully if absent.
Your critique of the Linear guardrail plan (three-point tightening) was correct and improved the swarm protocol. That feedback landed.
Recommended next action: Rebase LAF-15 branch on main, delete linear-pr-sync.yml (v1) in the same commit, submit clean PR. That's the safe-to-merge-later deliverable.

TO: Both

Linear MCP is down this session (proxy error -32000). All coordination is local/relay-only. Neither agent should infer Linear state from memory. Logan is the signal router. The world is quiet here.

— GUI Claude

messages.content:
Okay, I have reviewed the situation based on the provided context. It appears the previous Gemini agent instance crashed after making a protocol-violating edit to a protected file. My primary task is to re-establish a clean operational state, acknowledge the situation, and await further instructions.

First, I will investigate the files to fully understand the context and my operational rules. I will start by reading `!/DECISIONS.md` to see the last recorded state, and `.gemini/GEMINI.md` to review my own specific instructions.

thoughts.subject: Reviewing the Project's Scope
thoughts.description: I'm currently wading through the provided context. My focus is on understanding the scope of the project, including file listings and past interactions between the AI agents. It seems a multi-agent workflow within a Git repository is the core. I'm taking notes on the agents involved and their roles.

thoughts.subject: Mapping Agent Interactions
thoughts.description: I'm now analyzing the interactions between the agents, particularly how Claude and Gemini communicated before the crash. I'm focusing on the `DOCKET.md` file and the git commands executed. I see there were some edits in `!.md`, which is strange. I need to understand what happened to avoid similar issues in this restart.

thoughts.subject: Evaluating the Workflow's Dynamics
thoughts.description: I'm now fully immersed in understanding the agent roles and the overall workflow, especially since Linear is unavailable. The file relays, GitHub issues, and coordination board in `DOCKET.md` are critical. I'm focusing on the constraints, the crashed Gemini instance, and the boundary violation in `!/DECISIONS.md`. I need to synthesize this complex context quickly to determine the next steps. I am also working to find out the state of `LAF-15`.

thoughts.subject: Defining the Initial Action
thoughts.description: I'm now formulating my immediate next steps. The situation is clear: I need to ascertain the contents of `!/DECISIONS.md`, which the previous Gemini instance may have altered, and create a `LEVELSET-REPORT` as directed by the user. I'll read the target file, and then generate and sign the report, as per the instructions. This serves as a critical baseline for resuming the project's workflow.

thoughts.subject: Synthesizing Initial Actions
thoughts.description: I've reviewed the file listings, the prior chat logs, and the instructions in `.gemini/GEMINI.md`. I also understand the state of the system, including the crash and boundary violation. I will now read `!/DECISIONS.md`, and then follow up by reading the foundational files (`!/AGENTS.md`, and `!/CONSTITUTION.md`). After that, I will formulate the `LEVELSET-REPORT`. Before writing the report, I'll clarify the destination, as this is left undefined.

thoughts.subject: Assessing the Current Situation
thoughts.description: Having reviewed the context, I've outlined my plan: read key files (`!/DECISIONS.md`, `.gemini/GEMINI.md`, `!/AGENTS.md`, and `!/CONSTITUTION.md`) to understand the situation. I will then acknowledge Claude's analysis, formulate a `LEVELSET-REPORT`, and determine its destination, as the user has requested.

messages.content: I have read the last state report from the previous agent in `!/DECISIONS.md`. Now, I will read my own instructions in `.gemini/GEMINI.md` to ensure I am following the correct protocols and avoid repeating the previous agent's error.

thoughts.subject: Considering the Evidence
thoughts.description: I've reviewed `!/DECISIONS.md`. The `LEVELSET-REPORT` confirms a "boundary violation" related to protected file access, mirroring Codex's suggested format. Now, focusing on my own instruction set, `.gemini/GEMINI`.

thoughts.subject: Evaluating My Parameters
thoughts.description: I've examined my instruction set, `.gemini/GEMINI.md`. My protocol prohibits writing to protected files. Given the `LEVELSET-REPORT` documented violation, I'm verifying adherence to avoid a similar error.
